channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Pro-Palestinian protesters march to stadium ahead of World Cup qualifier between Norway and Israel
item.description: OSLO, Norway (AP) — Pro-Palestinian protesters lit flares and waved flags as they marched to the stadium ahead of the World Cup qualifier between Norway and Israel on Saturday. Public broadcaster NRK said around 1,000 protesters arrived at the Ullevaal Stadion in Oslo.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Drone footage above Gaza City shows the toll of 2 years of war
item.description: GAZA CITY, Gaza Strip (AP) — New drone footage shows what remains of Gaza City after two years of war and as a ceasefire holds in its second day. The footage taken by The Associated Press on Saturday shows few buildings still standing in the Tal al-Hawa neighborhood. The rest appear to be gutted.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Ivory Coast arrests 237 protesters amid rising tensions before presidential election
item.description: ABIDJAN, Ivory Coast (AP) — At least 237 people were arrested Saturday in Ivory Coast during a protest against what activists called the country's authoritarian drift, according to a statement by the Minister of the Interior and Security on national television.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: France's re-appointed prime minister Lecornu calls for calm amid political chaos
item.description: L'HAY-LES-ROSES, France (AP) — France’s newly re-appointed prime minister acknowledged Saturday that there weren’t ″a lot of candidates″ for his job — and that he might not last long in the post given the country’s deep political divides.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Korea displays new long-range missile at a military parade celebrating Kim Jong Un
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — At a massive military parade attended by foreign leaders, North Korean leader Kim Jong Un rolled out his nuclear-armed military’s most powerful weapons, including a new intercontinental ballistic missile he may be preparing to test in coming weeks.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG and HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Power restored to 800,000 in Kyiv after major Russian strikes on Ukraine's energy grid
item.description: Power was restored to over 800,000 residents in Kyiv on Saturday, a day after Russia launched major attacks on the Ukrainian power grid that caused blackouts across much of the country, and European leaders agreed to proceed toward using hundreds of billions of frozen Russian assets to support Ukrai
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel, amid Gaza ceasefire, kills at least 1 in overnight strikes on Lebanon
item.description: The Israeli military launched airstrikes on Lebanon overnight targeting Hezbollah and killing at least one person. The attack on the village of Msayleh also destroyed a highway that connects Lebanon to Beirut, the Lebanese Health Ministry said, according to The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Palestinians return to ruins and US troops land in Israel as Gaza ceasefire holds
item.description: Tens of thousands of displaced Palestinians are returning to neighborhoods in Gaza as a ceasefire between Israel and Hamas is in its second day.
item.dc:creator: Abdel Kareem Hana, Sam Metz, Sarah El Deeb And Sam Mednick, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Greece's famed Parthenon free of scaffolding for first time in decades
item.description: ATHENS, Greece (AP) — Walking along the broad pedestrian street that runs along the base of Athens’ famed Acropolis Hill, visitors can now enjoy something not seen in decades: an unobstructed, scaffolding-free view of the Parthenon temple. The scaffolding set up along the 5th century B.C.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel rejects freeing from prison the most popular Palestinian leader
item.description: RAMALLAH, West Bank (AP) — The most popular and potentially unifying Palestinian leader — Marwan Barghouti — is not among the prisoners Israel intends to free in exchange for hostages held by Hamas under the new Gaza ceasefire deal.
item.dc:creator: LEE KEATH, JULIA FRANKEL and JALAL BWAITAL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Latest: Tens of thousands of Palestinians in Gaza return north as ceasefire takes effect
item.description: Tens of thousands of Palestinians in Gaza were starting to walk north to return to their homes Friday, after the Israeli military said the ceasefire agreement with Hamas came into effect at noon local time and that its soldiers were withdrawing to the agreed-upon deployment lines.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump announces 100 percent tariff on China in response to rare earth controls
item.description: President Trump announced Friday he will raise tariffs on China in response to a move from Beijing to tighten its control over certain critical minerals and rare earth elements. Trump announced on Truth Social that he would impose a tariff of 100 percent on Chinese goods beginning Nov. 1 or sooner.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: US sends CENTCOM troops to Israel for Trump-brokered Hamas ceasefire monitoring
item.description: Adm. Brad Cooper said U.S. Central Command troops will establish a ceasefire monitoring center in Israel as the President Donald Trump-brokered peace deal between Israel and Hamas begins.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nobel Peace Prize bets on Polymarket raise leak concerns, prompts probe
item.description: The last-minute bets, first reported by local outlets, are a cause for concern for the Norwegian officials in charge of awarding the honor
item.dc:creator: Zach Kaplan, NewsNation

item.title: Putin praises Trump's peace efforts despite failing to win the Nobel prize
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russian President Vladimir Putin on Friday praised U.S. President Donald Trump's peace efforts, despite not winning a Nobel, and voiced hope for securing an agreement to extend a pivotal nuclear arms pact with Washington for another year.
item.dc:creator: VLADIMIR ISACHENKOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nobel winner dedicates peace prize to people of Venezuela, Trump
item.description: This year's Nobel Peace Prize winner, Venezuelan opposition leader María Corina Machado, has dedicated her award to the people of her country and to President Trump for his "decisive support” for democracy in Venezuela.
item.dc:creator: Surina Venkat, The Hill

item.title: Trump threatens countermeasures after China tightens grip on rare earths, says 'no reason' to meet Xi
item.description: President Trump on Friday threatened an increase in tariffs on China and other potential countermeasures after Beijing tightened control over rare earth elements and critical mineral exports.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Arctic seals, more than half of bird species on latest threatened list
item.description: While many animals are increasingly at risk of disappearing forever, the updated list shows how species can come back from the brink with dedicated effort.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel's military says Gaza ceasefire now in effect
item.description: Israel's military announced early Friday that the ceasefire in Gaza is now in effect, after Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's Cabinet approved the first phase of President Trump's 20-point peace plan.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Burkina Faso rejects proposal to accept deportees from the US
item.description: DAKAR, Senegal (AP) — Burkina Faso says it has refused a proposal from the Trump administration to accept deportees from the United States. The West African country was asked whether it would accept non-citizens expelled by the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MARK BANCHEREAU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NATO begins a big nuclear exercise next week. Protecting the weapons is a key part of the drill
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — NATO will hold its major annual nuclear exercise next week, the alliance’s chief announced Friday, with an important part of the drill to focus on protecting the weapons before they're ever used.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Opposition activist María Corina Machado of Venezuela wins the Nobel Peace Prize
item.description: The former opposition presidential candidate in Venezuela was lauded for being a “key, unifying figure in a political opposition that was once deeply divided — an opposition that found common ground in the demand for free elections and representative government.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russian strikes wound at least 20 in Kyiv as child killed in separate attack
item.description: The Russian strikes had targeted civilian and energy infrastructure as Ukraine prepared for falling winter temperatures, Zelenskyy said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: South Korean investigators indict Unification Church leader over alleged ties to ex-first lady
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korean investigators on Friday indicted the 82-year-old leader of the Unification Church, Hak Ja Han, over allegations that she instructed church officials to bribe the wife of jailed former President Yoon Suk Yeol and a conservative lawmaker close to him.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The West Bank's dwindling Palestinian Christian communities continue to struggle amid violence
item.description: TAYBEH, West Bank (AP) — Early on Sundays, bells call the faithful to worship at the three churches in this hilltop village that the Gospel narrates Jesus visited. It is now the last entirely Christian one in the occupied West Bank.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli Cabinet approves Trump's plan for Gaza ceasefire and release of hostages held by Hamas
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Israel’s Cabinet early Friday approved President Donald Trump’s plan for a ceasefire in the Gaza Strip and the release of all the remaining hostages held by Hamas, a key step toward ending a ruinous two-year war that has destabilized the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, MELANIE LIDMAN and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Exclusive: Israeli ambassador says no peace in Gaza unless Hamas hands over all 48 hostages, disarms
item.description: Israel's Ambassador Yechiel Leiter warns the Gaza war will continue unless Hamas returns all hostages and completely disarms under the new peace agreement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: World leaders express hope after Israel and Hamas agreed to first phase of plan to end Gaza war
item.description: ATHENS, Greece (AP) — World leaders on Thursday expressed hope for peace and urged Israel and Hamas to fulfill their commitments in the hours after U.S.
item.dc:creator: ELENA BECATOROS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Anti-Hamas Gaza militias reject terror group, declare support for Trump’s peace plan
item.description: Support for a peace plan grows as negotiators work in Cairo to seal the deal, with Gazan militias backing Trump’s proposal amid fighting and calls for new local governance.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Greta Thunberg slammed for using image of starved Israeli hostage to show Palestinians suffering in Gaza
item.description: Greta Thunberg is being criticized after using a photo of starving hostage Evyatar David in an Instagram post claiming to show Palestinians suffering.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Priscilla nears major hurricane status in Pacific as tropical storm swirls in Atlantic
item.description: Just off the west coast of Mexico, Priscilla was spinning with maximum sustained winds around 105 mph and moving northwest at 9 mph.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Greta Thunberg is among flotilla activists deported from Israel. Others remain in prison
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israeli authorities said Monday they deported to Greece and Slovakia another 171 people detained for taking part in a Gaza-bound aid flotilla, including prominent Swedish activist Greta Thunberg.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN and RENATA BRITO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Ukraine says it struck Russian ammunition plant, oil terminal and weapons depot
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Long-range Ukrainian drones and missiles hit a major Russian ammunition plant, a key oil terminal and an important weapons depot behind the front line, Ukraine's president and military said Monday, as Kyiv cranked up pressure on Moscow’s military logistics.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Rescuers in India search for missing after landslides kill 24 in Darjeeling region
item.description: NEW DELHI (AP) — Rescuers were scouring India's northeastern tea-growing hill district of Darjeeling on Monday for those reported missing a day after landslides triggered by heavy downpours killed at least 24 people, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Japan’s ruling party elects Sanae Takaichi as new leader, likely to become first female PM
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japan’s governing party on Saturday elected former Economic Security Minister Sanae Takaichi, a hard-line ultra-conservative and China hawk, as its new leader, making her likely to become the country’s first female prime minister.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, WGN 9
